briefdescription.para: run() is started by startQueue() in a separate thread and takes care of the queue execution (submits and collects data packets to and from the robot per cycle)
Can be used to set the number of degrees of freedom.
This method can be used to initialize custom parts of the ControlQueue implementation before the queue is started by startQueue().

The control queue collects the data about the actually executed trajectory during a point to point movement. This is automatically done by jointPtp(). jointPtp() calls the jointPtpInternal() function that actually plans the movement in joint space and executes it.
This method is called by the run() method once per clock cycle and actually submits the command about the next desired joint pose to the robot.
This method is called by the run() method once per clock cycle and actually submits the command about the next desired Cartesian pose to the robot.
This method is called by switchMode() and actually sends the control mode command to the robot.

The control queue collects the data about the actually executed trajectory during a point to point movement. This is automatically done by cartesianPtp(). cartesianPtp() calls the cartPtpInternal() function that actually plans the movement in Cartesian space and executes it.
This method can be used to add some additional initialization behavior when the queue is started. If no additional initialization is required, the method can be left empty.

This method determines, if the queue execetion should be stopped while ptp commands are executed (this is typically the case when ptp is done outside of the control queue implementation). If two different controls interfere it can result in dangerous behaviour.

simplesect.para: true if the queue should be stopped during ptp, false otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (StorageSingleton &storage, std::string robotName, std::string className, int degreesOfFreedom, double desiredCycleTime)
memberdef.name: ControlQueue

briefdescription.para: Constructor taking the robot dependent degrees of freedom (this constructor should be used for installing a new control queue)

parameterdescription.para: number of robots degrees of freedom

briefdescription.para: Sets the queue cycle time.

parameterdescription.para: time to wait between two cycles

briefdescription.para: Starts new thread to control the robot with real time capability.
Returns the time between two cycles.
Returns whether or not the queue is currently running.

simplesect.para: true if the queue is started, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Stops the queue.
With setNextTrajectory() a complete trajectory can be added to the queue. The trajectory will be executed after all other added commands (already contained in the queue) were executed.
Adds next joint position to the end of the queue (can be used for trajectory following)
Adds next cartesian position to the end of the queue (can be used for trajectory following)
Switches robot modes. A state might be a real time command mode or monitoring mode.
Stops current mode and switches back to default mode (e.g. monitoring mode)
Blocks, if more than the defined maximum element count is in the queue.

parameterdescription.para: maximum number of joints in queue

briefdescription.para: If this is set, the robot moves to the desired position by jointPtp() before the queue is started.
Implements simple point to point movement in joint space (blocks until target reached)
Implements simple point to point movement in joint space (not blocking)
Implements simple point to point movement in cartesian space.
Implements simple point to point movement in cartesian space.

parameterdescription.para: the point to point movement is stopped if the observed force exceeds this value (in Newton)

briefdescription.para: Implements simple point to point movement in cartesian space (does not block until the position is reached)
Changes the expected load data of the robot (e.g. can be used whenever robot picks up an object). Some robots might require this information in order to avoid control problems.

parameterdescription.para: mass of the picked up object
position of the objects center of gravity relative to the manipulator

memberdef.argsstring: (float cpstiffnessxyz, float cpstiffnessabc, float cpdamping, float cpmaxdelta, float maxforce, float axismaxdeltatrq)=0
memberdef.name: setStiffness

briefdescription.para: Sets certain stiffness parameters in cartesian space (if the robot supports this) according to a mass spring damper system model.

parameterdescription.para: stiffness of the robot in the cartesian space
stiffness of the rotational axis of the tool mounting point in cartesian space
damping of the robots axis in cartesian space
maximum allows deviation in cartesian space

briefdescription.para: Returns the current robot position in cartesian space.

simplesect.para: current pose of the robot in Cartesian space

briefdescription.para: Returns current robot position in cartesian space.

simplesect.para: current pose of the robot in Cartesian space

briefdescription.para: Returns the robot joints the robot has been directly before starting command mode.

simplesect.para: The starting joints set int setStartingJoints()

briefdescription.para: Returns joints if the robot is in command mode.

simplesect.para: The current joint pose of the robot

briefdescription.para: Returns the forces measured for the robot joints.

simplesect.para: The current forces measured at the robot joints (in Newton)

briefdescription.para: Returns the forces and torques measured at the robot end-effector.

simplesect.para: The current forces and torques (in Newton and Newton / meter)

briefdescription.para: Returns the forces and torques measured at the robot end-effector.

simplesect.para: The current forces and torques (in Newton and Newton / meter)

briefdescription.para: Sets the maximum deviation in joint space that is tolerated for a point to point movement.
Returns true if the command mode initialization is done.
Returns the robot name with escaped special characaters (e.g. white spaces)
Returns the robot joint names.
Returns currently measured absolute force on the end-effector.
Prevents the queue from printing debug information to the terminal.
Makes the queue printing debug information to the terminal.

If the rollback mode is started by startRollBackMode() the robot can stop the current execution and move backwards the same trajectory it came. Therefore it can move back in time for a given time (in seconds)
Stops the rollback mode (see startRollBackMode())

Starts the rollback mode. When the function rollBack() is called the robot can roll back the current trajectory for at most a the given time (in seconds). This mode is only available in joint space execution.
Indicates whether or not the queue is printing debug information to the terminal (see shutUp()).

simplesect.para: false if the queue is printing debug information to the terminal, true otherwise

briefdescription.para: Returns the current robot control mode (see switchMode())

memberdef.type: std::vector< std::pair< long long int, arma::vec > >
memberdef.definition: vector< std::pair< long long int, arma::vec > > kukadu::ControlQueue::loadData
memberdef.argsstring: (long long int startTime, long long int endTime, long long maxTotalDuration=3600000, long long int maxTimeStepDifference=5000)
memberdef.name: loadData

briefdescription.para: Interface between the kukadu software stack and the robot hardware. If kukadu shall support another robot, the abstract methods need to be implemented according to the documentation. Then the kukadu software stack is available for usage with the robot.

detaileddescription.para: The ControlQueue interface is the bridge between all implemented control methods (DMPs, ...) and the robot hardware. It supports functionlity such as trajectory following, simple point to point planning or security mechanisms such as maximum force values for execution.
